description: Structured form of the 8 operations defined in SKILL.md §4. Each operation is a primitive a coder-agent can invoke. Trigger → Action → Output → Evidence. Designed for downstream skill-fusion: an aggregator skill can include these by id without re-distilling.

operations.name: wire_one_shot_verifier
operations.trigger: User wants the agent to verify once after editing; no loop yet, but a success command exists.
operations.action: Run `<lint> && <type> && <test>` as one combined shell command. Capture three-tuple (stdout, stderr, exit_code). Set timeout (120s default) and NO_COLOR=1 in env.
operations.output: Pass/fail signal. If fail, raw output ready for distillation by OP-2.
evidence: aider/lint-test: 'Aider will try and fix any errors if the command returns a non-zero exit code.'

concrete_commands.go: gofmt -l . | (! grep .) && go vet ./... && go test ./...
concrete_commands.rust: cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo test

anti_patterns: Running lint and test as separate loops (doubles iter cost)
Not setting NO_COLOR=1 (ANSI burns tokens)
No timeout (one infinite test halts the agent)

operations.name: format_feedback_under_2k_tokens
operations.trigger: Verifier failed; about to construct the next prompt for the LM.
operations.action: Extract first failure block. Take last frame of traceback. Anchor to `git diff HEAD~1 --name-only -U0`. Strip ANSI, coverage summaries, deprecation warnings. Hard cap output at ~2000 tokens.
operations.output: A digest message of (a) failing item, (b) error class + message, (c) file:line, (d) anchor 'your last edit touched X', (e) optional one-line hypothesis.
evidence: aider/edit-errors: 'Above about 25k tokens of context, most models start to become distracted.'
External 2025 SWE research: 'noise in tool results degrades patch quality more than model choice within a class.'
anti_patterns: Dumping raw 4000-line pytest output
Dropping the file:line (model can't locate the bug)
Adding 'please fix this carefully' (imperatives hurt some models)
Concatenating stdout+stderr without labels

operations.name: bound_loop_with_cap_and_stall_detector
operations.trigger: About to enter or continue a fix loop.
operations.action: Set MAX_ITERS (3-5 for interactive use; 50-100 for SWE-Bench batch). Track last error string; if exact-match repeats, break early (stall). Enforce total wall-clock cap as outer guard.
operations.output: A loop with explicit termination conditions, never `while True`. Returns one of: Success(iters=N), Stall(reason='same error twice'), Exhausted(max_iters=N), Timeout(seconds=N), EnvFailure(class).
evidence: oh/6357: OpenHands SWE-Bench infinite-loop on context overflow (canonical failure case).
langgraph/recursion: 'Hitting recursion_limit indicates an underlying design flaw.'
anti_patterns: No iter cap (`while True`)
Cap too high (10+ for interactive — burns money on stuck cases)
No stall detector (loops forever on same error)

operations.name: commit_per_iteration
operations.trigger: Agent has applied an edit; before re-running verifier.
operations.action: `git add -A && git commit -m "agent[iter N/M]: <one-line summary>"`. Never `--amend`. If working tree has uncommitted user changes, stash first.
operations.output: A bisectable audit trail. Each iter is recoverable via `git reset --hard HEAD~K`.
evidence: aider/git: per-edit auto-commit is on by default; --no-auto-commits disables /undo trail.
cline/auto-approve: per-step approval is the IDE analog of per-edit commit.
anti_patterns: Using `--amend` between iters (loses bisect)
Disabling --auto-commits 'to keep history clean' (rebase post-loop instead)
Single commit at loop exit (no way to recover iter K if K+1 broke things)

operations.name: detect_success_without_false_positives
operations.action: Confirm three conditions: (a) tests were actually collected (e.g. pytest exit 5 ≠ success; check `pytest --collect-only -q | wc -l`), (b) no test was newly @skip/@xfail/disabled in the last iter's commit, (c) the verifier command itself does not contain `|| true` suppression. Run `git log -p -1 -- '*test*'` to check (b).
operations.output: Trusted 'green' signal, or 'suspicious_pass' tag with details for human review.
evidence: pytest/exit-codes: exit 5 = 'no tests collected' = silent false positive.
Empirical: agents commonly 'pass' by adding @pytest.mark.skip or removing assertions.
anti_patterns: Treating exit 0 as ground truth without diff check
Allowing `pytest -k <pattern>` in --test-cmd that might match zero tests
Running with `--continue-on-error` flags that mask failures

operations.name: classify_and_escalate_env_failures
operations.action: Maintain a regex classifier. If matched AND the agent's edits don't touch the affected module, classify as `env_failure`. Do NOT re-prompt the LM. Exit loop with message: 'Verifier failed with what looks like an environment issue (<error>). Agent did not edit further. Please fix the env and re-run.' Allow ONE retry for flakes; twice = escalate.
operations.output: Loop exits to human; agent is not asked to fix `apt-get install` problems by editing source.
evidence: swe-gym: SWE-Gym docs split infra-setup phase from agent-iteration phase.
Empirical: agents asked to fix `ModuleNotFoundError: psycopg2` for missing libpq will rewrite imports until codebase is broken.
env_patterns: Could not find a version that satisfies
anti_patterns: Re-prompting the LM with 'No module named X' (it will fake-fix imports)
No retry budget (flaky network = false escalation)
Too many retries (papers over real infra issues)

operations.name: handle_partial_success
operations.trigger: 8 of 10 failing tests now pass; 2 remain.
operations.action: Format feedback with explicit progress framing: 'Of N tests previously failing, K now pass. K' still fail; details below.' Then show only the K' remaining failures. Reset stall detector — different error class = real progress.
operations.output: Loop continues on smaller error surface; model is not punished for prior fixes.
evidence: Empirical: models given 'you broke things' framing (without progress context) tend to revert good fixes.
Cognitive: anchoring to net delta keeps the model focused on remaining work.
anti_patterns: Treating any non-zero exit as 'failure' (loses progress signal)
Listing all original failures every iter (model sees same errors despite fix)
Not resetting stall detector after progress (false stall break)

operations.name: handle_auto_formatters_that_rewrite
operations.trigger: Verifier includes a formatter (`ruff format`, `prettier --write`, `gofmt -w`) that modifies files and returns non-zero on first pass.
operations.action: Wrap in a two-pass script: pass 1 writes/formats; pass 2 runs the same formatter in check-only mode for the actual verification signal. Only pass-2 exit code feeds the loop.
operations.output: Loop doesn't get stuck re-running the same successful format; spurious non-zero exits don't trigger LM edits.
evidence: aider/lint-test: explicit guidance — 'formatters that rewrite files and return non-zero need a double-run wrapper.'
operations.wrapper_template:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e
ruff format .          # pass 1: write
ruff format --check .  # pass 2: verify (this is the signal)
anti_patterns: Putting `ruff --fix` in --lint-cmd (the agent's edits compete with the fixer)
Single-pass formatter (loop interprets 'I changed files' as 'tests failed')

swe_bench_eval_loop.tweaks: Higher iter cap (50-100); skip OP-7 (each instance is independent); env failures are graded as instance failure, not human-escalation.

ops_requiring_user_work: OP-6 (treats env errors as code errors)

known_bugs: #6357 infinite loop on context overflow (OP-3 + context-truncation interaction)

claude_code_bash_tool.note: Naked Bash tool — full loop must be wrapped by user script or skill orchestration.

pairs_well_with: human-in-the-loop (when verifier passes but a sensitive change needs review)
bounded-loop (this skill specializes the general bounded-loop pattern for code edits)
tool-result-feedback (this skill is the code-edit instance of the general pattern)
repo-map (when the agent needs file context before editing in the loop)
conventions-pinning (formatter + lint rules belong in CONVENTIONS.md)
supersedes: 'wrap pytest in a while loop' folk patterns
ad-hoc shell scripts that call an LLM API and re-prompt with raw output
